subparts.text: What does PERMA stand for? i. Positive Emotions, Empathy, Relationships, Meaning, Actualisation ii. Positive Emotions, Engagement, Relationships, Meaning, Authenticity iii. Positive Emotions, Energy, Relationships, Mental Health, Achievement iv. Positive Emotions, Engagement, Relationships, Meaning, Achievement
________ proposed the hierarchy of needs. i. William James ii. David McClelland iii. Abraham Maslow iv. Albert Bandura

What does the term well-being include? i. Well-being includes good physical health, a happy state of mind ii. Well-being comprises living in a big house and an affluent area iii. Well-being includes being liked by all your peers iv. None of the above

If you had some spare money, spending money shopping might induce a sense of ________ while helping the needy would induce a sense of __________? i. happiness; contentment ii. pleasure; gratification iii. materialism; spirituality iv. temporary optimism; more long-lasting optimism
The pleasures are concerned with i. eudaimonia ii. the bodily senses and the emotions and positive perceptions iii. enacting personal strengths and virtues iv. both ‘a’ and ‘b’ above

Which of the following would be an example of a shortcut to the pleasures that might result in a neglect of the gratifications i. eating food that pleases the palate ii. watching TV/movies that have high entertainment value iii. drinking alcohol or taking recreational drugs iv. all of the above
Who is the father of Positive Psychology? i. Martin Seligman ii. Carl Rojers iii. Stevenson iv. None of the above

____________ refers to how well we are equipped to cope with the problems of life. i. Livability of environment ii. Life-ability of the person iii. Utility of life iv. Satisfaction i) Compared to pessimists, optimists tend to i. Be less able to cope with stress ii. Experience more distress iii. Doubt about solving the problem iv. Be confident about solving the problem j) Lottery winners are not significantly happier than those in a control group a year after winning the lottery. Which of the following features of our mindset supports the given statement? i. Our minds’ strongest intuitions are often totally wrong ii. Our minds don’t think in terms of absolutes; our minds judge to relative reference points iii. Our minds are built to get used to stuff iv. We don’t realize that our minds are built to get used to stuff k) According to positive psychology, happiness involves all of the following except i. Experiencing Pleasure ii. Having good wealth iii. Being engaged in an activity iv. Engaged in a meaningful activity l) The ecstatic kind of satisfaction characterized as a passing experience which is intense, pervasive and 'oceanic', is usually referred to as ____________ i. Pleasure ii. Part satisfaction iii. Top experience iv. Life satisfaction m) Creating a detailed strategy plan for your goals will help you to______ i. Understand and implement each task ii. Give you a start and end date iii. Identify your team and resources iv. All of the above n) Considering the research studies, which of the options below closely aligns with the comparison of PREDICTED emotions to ACTUAL emotions after receiving bad news like getting a bad grade or getting turned down for a job offer. i. 😐 Neutral , 😐 Neutral ii. 😐 Neutral , 😭 Super Sad iii. 😭 Super Sad , 😭 Super Sad iv. 😭 Super Sad , 😐 Neutral o) Which of the following is an example of intrinsic motivation? i. Completing the project to impress the teacher ii. Playing a musical instrument for personal enjoyment iii. Studying to avoid being scolded iv. Participating in a quiz for money p) When you do a google search for "[Social Media Platform] makes me feel” you get AutoFill searches as given below. Which of the following features of our mind best fits these patterns? i. Our minds’ strongest intuitions are often totally wrong ii. Our minds don’t think in terms of absolutes; our minds judge to relative reference points iii. Our minds are built to get used to stuff iv. We don’t realize that our minds are built to get used to stuff q) The word affect refers to? i. Emotions ii. Drives iii. Actively pursuing your goals iv. The opposite of cause r) On average, people would rather make less if their peers earned less over being paid more if their peers made more. Which of the following features of our mindset supports the given statement? i. Our minds’ strongest intuitions are often totally wrong ii. Our minds don’t think in terms of absolutes; our minds judge to relative reference points iii. Our minds are built to get used to stuff iv. We don’t realize that our minds are built to get used to stuff s) Being able to successfully recover from negative life events is known as? i. Resilience ii. Positive effect iii. Coping iv. None of the above t) What is the percentage breakdown of the factors that determine our general happiness? i. 50% Genetic Setpoint, 50% Life Circumstances ii. 40% Genetic Setpoint, 60% Life Circumstances iii. 50% Genetic Setpoint, 10% Life Circumstances, and 40% Our Intentional Actions and Thoughts iv. 50% Genetic Setpoint, 40% Life Circumstances, and 10% Our Intentional Actions and Thoughts

questions.question_text: Using Veehoven’s life quality matrix, analyze the factors affecting the life satisfaction of students of IGDTUW.

A student believes they are "not good at math" and avoids challenges in the subject. Apply the concept of a growth mindset to help them reframe their perspective and approach learning. How does fostering a growth mindset contribute to overall happiness?
What is happiness and discuss its main characteristics? Describe and examine critically the various theories of happiness?

What is “miswanting,” and how does it manifest in decision-making processes? Discuss real-life scenarios or experiments that highlight the drivers of miswanting and suggest practical ways to mitigate its effects.
Do you agree with the given statement “Social media is the junk food of social life” and why?
Reflect on your experience with the gratitude journalling. How might such a practice help someone cope with challenges?
Analyze a scenario where a person pursues short-term pleasure at the expense of long-term happiness. What steps could they take to shift focus from pleasure to sustainable happiness?
What do you understand by “subjective well-being”? Describe the factors impacting the well-being of a person.
